name: Pumpernickek
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 tb Dry milk, or dry buttermilk
1 1/2 ts Brotgewurtz (a German mixture of anis, fennel, and coriander - either whole seed or ground)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix all the ingredients together in a large bowl until well combined.
2. Let the dough rest for about an hour to allow the flavors to meld.
3. Shape the dough into a loaf and place it on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
4. Cover the loaf with a clean kitchen towel or aluminum foil and let it proof at room temperature for 8-12 hours, until doubled in size.
5. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).
6. Bake the bread for 30 minutes covered with foil.
7. Remove the foil and bake for an additional 20-25 minutes until golden brown on top.
8. Allow the bread to cool completely before slicing and serving.
This recipe will yield a dark, moist bread with a rich texture. It's perfect for making pumpernickel sandwiches or enjoying it plain. Enjoy!
